task_completion.description: Open the InventoryManager project in the IDE
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eclipse IDE is open with the 'InventoryManager' project visible and expanded in the Package Explorer or Project Explorer.

task_completion.description: Resolve all instances of unused imports
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible Java files (e.g., Product.java, InventoryService.java) do not contain unused import statements (like java.util.Date or java.sql.SQLException) at the top of the file.
Visible Java files show '@Override' annotations correctly placed above methods that override superclass or interface methods.

task_completion.description: Resolve raw type usage and unnecessary boxing
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible code shows parameterized types (e.g., 'List<String> tags' instead of 'List tags;') and primitive/direct assignments instead of boxing (e.g., '42' instead of 'Integer.valueOf(42)').

task_completion.description: Resolve String comparisons using '==' instead of '.equals()'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible code shows string comparisons using the '.equals()' method (e.g., 'sku.equals(otherSku)' or '"Default".equals(categoryName)') instead of the '==' operator.

task_completion.description: Ensure the final project contains zero errors and zero warnings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Problems' view tab in Eclipse is visible and shows '0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 others' (or is completely empty of warning/error items) for the InventoryManager project.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse IDE to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Eclipse IDE interface (e.g., using the Source > Clean Up menu, or editing files within the Eclipse editor) rather than using terminal commands like 'sed' or a basic text editor.

integrity.description: The Problems view genuinely reflects the project state
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Problems view is not artificially filtered to hide warnings/errors (filter icon should not indicate active exclusion of warnings).

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the source files
integrity.visual_evidence: The editor tabs do not have a dirty state asterisk (*) in the final screenshot, indicating changes were actually saved to disk.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project is located at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/InventoryManager. The VLM should verify the absence of the following specific code smells in the final state: unused imports (`java.util.Date`, `java.io.Serializable`, `java.sql.SQLException`), raw types (`List tags;`), string equality using `==` (`sku == otherSku`, `categoryName == "Default"`), and unnecessary boxing (`Integer.valueOf(42)`). The final state must show an empty Problems view in Eclipse indicating 0 errors and 0 warnings.
